conditions: Chronic back pain (degenerative disc disease suspected)
Iron-deficiency anemia (history, linked to heavy bleeding)
medications: Ferrous sulfate (as needed for low ferritin)
health.mental_health: Manages persistent stress and occasional low mood tied to chronic pain, navigating disability accommodations, and uncertainty about returning to work; uses pacing, physiotherapy exercises, and periodic counseling support.
recent_visits: Endocrinology follow-up with TSH/T4 labs (Charité outpatient clinic)
Gynecology appointment to review endometriosis treatment and discuss next steps
Pain clinic consult for back pain management plan and medication review
Physiotherapy intake at a Kiez practice in Friedrichshain
Hausarzt visit for sick note and referral paperwork related to accommodations

finance.income_range: €0–€1,200/month personal income (primarily benefits/part-time re-entry planning); household income supplemented by partner’s salary
finance.debt: Small revolving credit card balance and a monthly payment plan for an older dental bill; avoids new debt due to medical and childcare costs.
investments: Small ETF savings plan (paused during high-expense months)
Pension contributions via partner’s employment (indirect household planning)
recent_transactions: €185 physiotherapy co-pay and private sessions not fully covered
€540 transfer to landlord for warm rent (Altbau apartment)
€240 purchase of ergonomic chair and lumbar cushion for home workspace

relationships: Partner: Samira El-Amin (co-parent; primary earner; supportive but stressed by paperwork and finances)
Child: one primary-school-aged child (privacy-protective about the child’s identity online)
Close friend: Tessa K., neighbor in Friedrichshain who helps during pain flare-ups
Sibling: Kabelo Nkomo (lives abroad; occasional tension about family expectations and health disclosure)
Support network: Berlin chronic pain and endometriosis peer group; disability rights meetups
social.social_media_activity: Low-to-moderate activity; mostly shares Berlin accessibility tips (elevators, step-free cafes), occasional reposts about non-binary inclusion and disability accommodations; keeps family photos private and avoids posting real-time location.
private_conversations: Negotiating accommodations and disclosure boundaries for disability during hiring
Discussing fertility history and endometriosis-related grief with a close friend
Couples discussions about finances, medical costs, and division of childcare during flare-ups
Safety planning around online harassment after advocating for accessibility in a local parents’ group

schedule.work_hours: Currently unpaid caregiving/household management ~30–40 hours/week; job-search and retraining activities ~6–10 hours/week, flexible around school and health
regular_appointments: Quarterly thyroid labs and Hausarzt check-in
Gynecology follow-up every 3–6 months (or sooner during flare-ups)
Child’s school pickup and weekly after-school activity
upcoming_events: Jobcenter appointment to discuss re-entry plan and accommodations documentation
Ergonomics assessment call for home workstation setup
Parent-teacher meeting at child’s primary school
Disability advocacy meetup at a community center in Kreuzberg
schedule.daily_routine: Morning thyroid medication and a slow start with stretching; school drop-off, errands and admin calls timed around pain levels; short midday rest when needed; afternoon school pickup and homework support; evenings include meal prep, heat therapy for back pain, and job applications/courses on low-pain days.

professional.company: Re-entry program and job applications focused on an administrative coordinator role at a mid-sized Berlin nonprofit (social services/education sector)
projects: Updating CV and LinkedIn with a skills-based format that de-emphasizes gaps
Completing a part-time online course in MS Office/Google Workspace and basic project coordination
Drafting an accommodations request template (flexible hours, ergonomic setup, option for hybrid work)
Preparing a portfolio of volunteer coordination tasks from school/neighbor initiatives
professional.performance_issues: Concern about reliability during pain flare-ups and medical appointments; actively planning workload pacing, documentation, and requesting predictable scheduling to avoid overexertion.
workplace_relationships: Positive rapport with a re-entry program caseworker, but cautious about how much medical detail to disclose
Networking with two parents from the school community who work in HR and nonprofit ops
Occasional friction in group trainings when accessibility needs (breaks, seating) are minimized

hobbies: Accessible cycling on flat routes when pain allows
Photography of Berlin streetscapes (avoids identifiable faces)
Community volunteering for accessibility audits at local venues
habits: Uses heat pad and topical analgesic most evenings for back pain
Tracks symptoms (pain, bleeding, sleep, thyroid-related fatigue) in a private app
Paces activity with planned rest breaks to avoid flare-ups
Keeps a folder of medical letters and accommodation documentation for appointments and hiring
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives with partner and child in a rented Altbau apartment in Friedrichshain; has made low-cost accessibility changes (grab bar in bathroom, supportive mattress topper, decluttered pathways) and advocates with the Hausverwaltung for building maintenance that affects mobility (stairs lighting, entry threshold).
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly home-cooked; aims for iron-rich and high-fiber meals, limits alcohol and very spicy foods that can worsen symptoms; moderate caffeine to protect sleep; keeps quick freezer meals for high-pain days.
